description: Govern the public/internal vocabulary split so customer-facing surfaces use Onyx while owner/operator surfaces may use Mason where intended.

severity_levels.critical: Mason or Athena is visible in a customer/public-facing surface.
severity_levels.high: A customer/public-facing artifact is ambiguous and likely to leak an internal name.
severity_levels.medium: A non-customer artifact could become customer-visible without remediation.
severity_levels.low: An internal-only wording inconsistency or policy hygiene issue.

allowed_exceptions.reason: Owner-only Athena, Founder Mode, Live Docs, and internal operator artifacts may reference Mason.
Athena remains the owner/operator cockpit and may be referenced internally.

remediation_guidance.critical: Replace the leaked internal brand term with Onyx or an approved customer-safe label immediately, then rerun the audit.
remediation_guidance.high: Clarify the customer-facing copy so only Onyx or approved public labels remain visible.
remediation_guidance.medium: Review the artifact before it becomes customer-visible and convert internal names to public-safe vocabulary if needed.
remediation_guidance.low: Keep the wording consistent, but do not remove intentional Mason references from owner/internal surfaces.

public_facing_naming_rules: Customer, tenant, billing, support, and public-facing copy must use Onyx or approved public-safe labels only.
Do not expose Mason or Athena in customer-visible UI copy, exports, billing copy, recommendation copy, manuals, or workflow text.
If a customer-facing surface needs to describe background intelligence or automation, describe the capability without naming Mason.
internal_facing_naming_rules: Owner-only and internal operator surfaces may reference Mason, Athena, and Onyx where the distinction is useful.
Do not treat internal Mason references as leaks when the surface is explicitly owner-only or internal_operator.
